items.headline: Planet and Carbon Mapper sign agreement for new Tanager hyperspectral spacecraft
items.whyItMatters: Extends the hyperspectral franchise (methane/CO2 and materials detection) beyond Tanager-1, supporting a recurring sustainability/government data motion and a second differentiated data product alongside optical imagery.

items.headline: Planet reports Q4 and full-year FY2026: record $86.8M Q4 revenue (+~41% YoY), first full year of adjusted-EBITDA profitability, backlog +79% YoY to ~$900M
items.whyItMatters: Inflection quarter: first annual non-GAAP profit ($15.5M adj. EBITDA vs. an ~$11M loss prior year), record backlog and ~$640M cash de-risk the story and justify a re-rating; FY2027 revenue guide of $415-440M (~39% growth) signals durable acceleration.

items.headline: Planet to build 'world's first GPU-native AI engine for planetary intelligence' with NVIDIA
items.whyItMatters: Anchors the analytics/AI thesis to NVIDIA Blackwell/IGX Thor compute, promising near-real-time insight generation (management cited up to 100x speedups on parts of the codebase) and a higher-margin software layer on top of imagery.

items.headline: Planet selected as a prime under the Missile Defense Agency's $151B SHIELD IDIQ
items.whyItMatters: Establishes Planet as a prime (not just a data sub) on a marquee U.S. missile-defense vehicle for AI-enabled detections/warnings, expanding addressable defense budget and validating the analytics pivot for national-security buyers.

items.headline: Planet signs low nine-figure multi-year satellite-services deal with the Swedish Armed Forces (alongside ICEYE)
items.whyItMatters: Third sovereign satellite-services win in ~12 months (after Germany and Japan/JSAT), reinforcing the dedicated-capacity model for allied governments and adding meaningful multi-year backlog; co-award with SAR provider ICEYE shows optical+SAR allied procurement.

items.headline: Planet launches high-resolution Pelican-5 and Pelican-6 satellites plus 36 SuperDoves
items.whyItMatters: Demonstrates fleet-refresh execution and capacity growth: scaling the higher-value Pelican high-res tasking constellation while sustaining daily PlanetScope coverage, the supply side needed to convert sovereign backlog.

items.headline: Planet announces ~EUR 240M ($280M) multi-year German government satellite-services agreement
items.whyItMatters: Largest contract in Planet's history at signing; dedicated Pelican capacity plus PlanetScope/SkySat data and AI for European security, with revenue recognition starting ~Jan 2026 and ramping - a template for the sovereign 'satellite services' model.

items.headline: Planet and Anthropic partner to use Claude to turn satellite imagery into actionable insights
items.whyItMatters: Signals the LLM/analytics direction: pairing Planet's daily global archive with frontier AI for pattern recognition/anomaly detection, supporting a shift up the value chain from raw data to decisions.

items.headline: Planet signs $230M commercial agreement with SKY Perfect JSAT to build and operate 10 Pelican satellites
items.whyItMatters: Capital-light fleet expansion in Asia-Pacific: a customer-funded constellation (revenue recognized over ~7 years) that grows capacity and recurring services revenue without Planet bearing all the capex.

items.headline: Google's Project Suncatcher to launch Planet satellites carrying Google TPUs to test compute in space
items.whyItMatters: Optionality on a new high-profile growth vector: Planet builds the pilot spacecraft for Google's orbital-data-center experiment (launch targeted ~early 2027), extending the platform beyond imaging into space-based compute infrastructure.
